text.source_text: स्मृतिश्च शारीरपरमात्मनोर्भेदं दर्शयति — ‘ ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति । भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ’ (भ. गी. १८ । ६१) इत्याद्या ।
text.transliteration_iast: smṛtiśca śārīraparamātmanorbhedaṃ darśayati — ‘ īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe'rjuna tiṣṭhati | bhrāmayansarvabhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā ’ (bha. gī. 18 | 61) ityādyā |

translation.literal_translation: And smriti too shows difference between embodied self and Supreme Self, in passages such as: 'The Lord dwells in the heart-region of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings to revolve, mounted on machine-like structures, by maya.'
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya support line: smriti corroborates jiva-Ishvara distinction relevant to the adhikarana conclusion.
translation.interpretive_note: Cross-text reinforcement from Gita-style antaryami language.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Corroborative authority used to reinforce bheda already grounded in sruti reasoning.

lexical_notes.gloss: difference between individual self and Lord
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Doctrinal distinction cited from smriti to support non-identity in contemplative grammar.
Smrti functions not as primary source here, but as confirmatory strengthening of prior siddhanta.
The line’s function is to stabilize the current adhikarana decision before moving onward.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit sharira/jiva/upadhi precision in records 1.2.007-1.2.014.
